data.title: Migration did not auto-tag brainstorm:complete on PIL-bearing epics after plugin update
data.description:
## Observation

Running `/dso:sprint` with no args on 2026-04-18 showed only 2 epics with the `brainstorm:complete` tag, while 85 epics without the tag were hidden. Many of the hidden epics contain Problem/Intent/Lenses (PIL) fields from earlier brainstorm sessions and should have been auto-tagged `brainstorm:complete` when the PIL-guarded tag management CLI was installed.

Two possible root causes (either or both):

1. **Migration script did not run on plugin update** — when the plugin was updated to the version containing the PIL-guarded `brainstorm:complete` auto-tagger (epic 9213-2746), no migration ran to back-fill the tag on pre-existing epics that already satisfy the PIL guard.
2. **Migration script ran but missed PIL-bearing epics** — a migration ran but its selection logic did not correctly identify epics whose descriptions contain PIL fields and therefore qualify for the tag.

## Impact

- `/dso:sprint` interactive epic selection surfaces only 2 epics when ~85+ are actually ready.
- Users must run `/dso:brainstorm` on already-scrutinized epics to get the tag applied, duplicating work.
- Epic backlog appears artificially empty, masking sprint-ready work.

## Reproduction

1. Run `.claude/scripts/dso sprint-list-epics.sh --all --has-tag=brainstorm:complete` — returns 2 epics.
2. Run `.claude/scripts/dso sprint-list-epics.sh --all` — returns ~94 lines (87 non-BLOCKED).
3. Spot-check a hidden epic (e.g., `.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 736d-b957` vs a non-tagged one) — many have PIL-field descriptions but lack the tag.

## Investigation starting points

- Locate the migration script for epic 9213-2746 (PIL-guarded brainstorm:complete CLI) and check whether it includes a back-fill path.
- Check `update-artifacts` / plugin-update flow for hooks that should run migrations.
- Verify the PIL-detection logic against sample hidden epics.
